executive_summary: Defence Housing Australia (DHA) is a corporate Commonwealth entity and Government Business Enterprise that provides housing and housing-related services to ~61,189 permanent ADF members, a population forecast to grow to 80,000 by 2040. The 2025-26 Statement of Corporate Intent sets a clear, investment-heavy agenda built on four objectives: a modern, customer-centric operating model enabled by rapid digitisation; building an agile, scalable housing capability to support ADF growth; embedding ESG (including new Commonwealth climate disclosure obligations); and fiscal responsibility / long-term financial stability. The strongest BD signals are the end-to-end customer-interface digitisation program, the strategic provisioning / portfolio-growth ('case for growth') agenda, a new Asset Management program targeting repairs-and-maintenance cost reduction, and an explicit ESG baseline-and-strategy build. As a self-funded GBE under cost and capital-access pressure, DHA is a credible prospect for digital delivery, financial-sustainability/costing, program delivery and governance/assurance work — sized to a focused, commercially-disciplined organisation.

strategic_priorities.title: Digitise the customer and property operating model
strategic_priorities.description: DHA is committed to moving 'faster to deliver end to end digital services' across tenancy, postings, enquiry management, rent allowance, property management, online maintenance and inspections, with a multi-year roadmap of customer-interface digitisation projects from 2025-26 through 2028-29.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Digitise quickly – move faster to deliver end to end digital services that balance both customer satisfaction and improved commercial outcomes.

strategic_priorities.title: The case for growth — scalable housing capability
strategic_priorities.description: A deliberate shift towards DHA playing a larger role in the ADF's housing requirements, growing the owned/leased portfolio to meet ADF growth to 80,000 by 2040, developing new sourcing channels and product models, and embedding them into BAU.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The case for growth – a shift in strategic direction towards DHA playing a larger role in supporting the housing requirements of the ADF.

strategic_priorities.title: ESG baseline and strategy
strategic_priorities.description: DHA will establish an ESG baseline defining how existing programs contribute to ESG objectives, then write an ESG Strategy setting future activities and targets, while meeting EPBC Act compliance and new Commonwealth Climate Disclosure obligations.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Establish an ESG baseline that defines how DHA's existing programs and activities contribute to ESG objectives... Write an ESG Strategy that builds on the baseline.

strategic_priorities.title: Fiscal responsibility and asset-management cost reduction
strategic_priorities.description: DHA is pursuing strong value for money and long-term financial stability, including an Asset Management program to reduce repairs and maintenance costs, digitise asset accounting and condition assessments, and introduce strategic procurement, with a 3% return-on-equity target.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Asset Management program seeks to reduce repairs and maintenance costs, focusing on digitisation of asset accounting, and a bigger focus on preventive/programmed maintenance.

strategic_priorities.title: Formalise future housing needs with Defence
strategic_priorities.description: DHA will agree and formalise future housing needs through Future Services Agreement and Housing Policy projects to ensure certainty in housing supply against the Approved Provisioning Schedule.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Agree and formalise future housing needs with Defence to ensure certainty in housing supply.

reform_and_change_drivers: ADF workforce growth from ~61,189 to 80,000 members by 2040, aligned to the National Defence Strategy 2024, Defence Strategic Review 2023 and Defence Workforce Plan 2024-2034 — driving portfolio growth and provisioning demand.

Constrained housing markets with tight rental supply, low construction completions and falling greenfield land release, especially in priority regions (WA and northern Australia) flagged in the National Defence Strategy.
Access to capital pressure: DHA is self-funded through rents, fees, debt financing and land sales and must achieve a sustainable portfolio of owned and leased properties.
New mandatory Commonwealth Climate Disclosure obligations (developed by Finance) starting 2024-25 and EPBC Act compliance requirements.
Advances in technology including agentic AI and 'industrial agent' customer-service solutions reshaping the digital customer experience.
A planned holistic review of KPIs in 2025-26 to be reflected in the 2026-27 PBS and SCI.
Increased climate/disaster risk exposure for the portfolio as more ADF members are based in Northern Australia.
challenges_and_risks: Financial sustainability of a self-funded GBE that must fund portfolio growth while delivering a 3% return on equity — balancing service culture with commercial culture.
Securing leased properties in constrained, priority markets while keeping pace with ADF growth demand.
Repairs and maintenance cost pressure across a large owned/leased portfolio (~15,760 DHA owned/leased solutions).
Delivery and integration risk of a multi-year, end-to-end digitisation program touching tenancy, property management and finance systems.
Meeting new climate-disclosure and EPBC compliance obligations from a low ESG baseline ('it is early in DHA's trajectory').
Weak/newly-introduced performance measures — DHA itself is initiating a holistic KPI review, signalling the current framework is under question.
Strategic risk oversight: a KPI of zero strategic risks outside tolerance implies tight enterprise risk-control expectations.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver a multi-year program of customer-interface and property-management digitisation (tenancy, postings, enquiry, rent allowance, online maintenance, inspections) with new AI-enabled service capability.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A discovery and service-design engagement for the customer-interface digitisation roadmap, followed by solutions implementation and integration of online maintenance, inspections and rent-allowance workflows — with business/process analysis to redesign the underlying operating model. Can extend to evaluation of off-the-shelf 'industrial agent' AI customer-service tools.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names digitisation as the top strategic direction with a sequenced 2025-26 to 2028-29 project pipeline across specific functions, and explicitly flags agentic AI / industrial agents as an enabler. The firm's ability to build and implement (not just advise) fits this multi-year demand directly.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Stand up scalable housing capability and new sourcing channels/product models to meet ADF growth to 80,000, then embed them into BAU.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program/PMO setup and embedded delivery support for the 'case for growth' strategic-provisioning initiative, including a benefits-management framework and change management to embed new sourcing channels into BAU operations.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan describes a deliberate strategic shift ('the case for growth') with named multi-year activities (develop new sourcing channels and product model, embed into BAU). This is a discrete, time-bound investment program that benefits from delivery discipline and assurance.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Sustain self-funded operations and portfolio growth under capital-access pressure while delivering a 3% return on equity and long-term financial stability.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review and financing-options assessment of the portfolio-growth strategy (buy/lease/build mix, debt financing, land sales), including a financial model linking provisioning scenarios to return-on-equity and long-term portfolio objectives.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Strategic Driver 2 ('Identification of financing options') and Objective 4 explicitly raise access to capital, self-funding constraints and the need for value-for-money. As a corporate GBE under cost pressure, this is a strong financial-advisory fit.

opportunity_areas.area: Asset management costing and strategic procurement
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Reduce repairs and maintenance costs, digitise asset accounting and condition assessments, and introduce strategic procurement under the Asset Management program.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An activity-based costing model for repairs and maintenance and an asset-cost baseline to underpin the move to preventive/programmed maintenance, paired with a strategic procurement approach for maintenance contracting.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Objective 4 names an Asset Management program targeting R&M cost reduction, asset-accounting digitisation and strategic procurement — concrete, costable activities. A secondary Procurement, Probity & Contracts angle applies to the maintenance procurement.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Establish an ESG baseline defining how existing programs contribute to ESG objectives, then write an ESG Strategy with future activities and targets, while meeting climate-disclosure and EPBC obligations.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short advisory engagement to establish the ESG baseline and author the ESG Strategy (activities, targets, governance), aligned to Commonwealth Climate Disclosure Policy requirements and EPBC compliance.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Objective 3 explicitly sequences a baseline then a written strategy in 2025-26, and the plan notes DHA is early in its ESG trajectory — a clearly-scoped, proportionate advisory piece.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Run a holistic review of KPIs in 2025-26 to feed the 2026-27 PBS and SCI.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA- and RMG 134-aligned performance-measure redesign: review existing KPIs, design outcome-oriented measures and targets linked to the Enterprise Performance Framework, ready for the 2026-27 PBS/SCI.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states 'In 2025-26, we are initiating a holistic review of our KPIs' — an explicit, time-bound need that maps cleanly to a performance-framework engagement.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Meet new mandatory Commonwealth Climate Disclosure obligations (from 2024-25 Annual Report) and manage rising portfolio climate/disaster risk, especially in Northern Australia.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent assurance / readiness health-check over DHA's climate disclosures and the data and controls underpinning Scope 1-3 emissions reporting, plus assurance of disaster-resilience risk treatments for the portfolio.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to climate disclosures under the Commonwealth Climate Disclosure Policy in the Annual Report and flags increased climate risk; new disclosure regimes commonly require independent assurance of process and data.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the executive owner of the digitisation workstream (Managing Director's office / head of customer and property services) with a discovery-and-delivery capability statement covering service design, build, integration and AI customer-service tooling.
recommended_actions.rationale: Digitisation is the named top priority with a sequenced multi-year project pipeline; the firm's build-and-implement capability is a differentiated fit and the 2025-26 projects are imminent.

recommended_actions.action: Offer a scoped proposal for the 2025-26 holistic KPI review to the corporate/performance function ahead of the 2026-27 PBS/SCI drafting cycle.
recommended_actions.rationale: DHA has explicitly committed to this review this year; it is a small, well-defined engagement that opens a door to broader strategy and governance work and is time-bound to the budget cycle.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a financial-sustainability / financing-options thought piece for the CFO and Board Audit and Risk Committee (BARC) linking portfolio growth to return-on-equity and capital constraints.
recommended_actions.rationale: Self-funding and access to capital are named strategic drivers for a growth agenda; a credible costing/financial perspective positions the firm for the financing and asset-management opportunities.

recommended_actions.action: Engage the ESG / sustainability lead with a baseline-and-strategy proposal aligned to the Commonwealth Climate Disclosure Policy, framed as a fast, proportionate engagement.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan sequences an ESG baseline then strategy in 2025-26 from a low maturity base — a near-term, clearly-scoped need.

recommended_actions.action: Run independent research to confirm current ANAO, Senate Estimates and media positions on DHA before first meetings.
recommended_actions.rationale: External research could not be completed in this brief; verifying assurance and parliamentary signals will sharpen the pitch and surface any program risk worth addressing.

engagement_targets: Managing Director's office (the only executive director; responsible for conducting DHA's affairs)
Head of customer / property services function (owns the digitisation and property-management activities)
Chief Financial Officer / finance function (financing options, asset-management costing, return-on-equity)
Corporate / strategy and performance function (KPI review, EPF, SCI/PBS drafting)
ESG / sustainability lead (ESG baseline and strategy, climate disclosures)
Board Audit and Risk Committee (BARC) (strategic risk oversight, assurance)
Property and Services Committee of the Board (portfolio growth and provisioning)
talking_points: Your end-to-end digitisation roadmap spans tenancy, property management and rent allowance over four years — we don't just advise, we design, build and integrate, and can help you evaluate off-the-shelf AI customer-service agents to lower implementation cost.

You've committed to a holistic KPI review in 2025-26 feeding the 2026-27 PBS and SCI — we can deliver a PGPA-aligned performance-measure redesign mapped to your Enterprise Performance Framework on that timeline.

As a self-funded GBE pursuing growth to support an ADF heading to 80,000, the financing-options and return-on-equity equation is central — we can model provisioning scenarios against your capital constraints.

Your Asset Management program targets repairs-and-maintenance cost reduction and asset-accounting digitisation — an activity-based costing baseline can quantify the savings from preventive/programmed maintenance and underpin strategic procurement.

You're early in your ESG trajectory with a baseline and strategy due this year against new Commonwealth climate-disclosure obligations — we can deliver a proportionate, disclosure-aligned ESG baseline and strategy quickly.
caveats: This is a Statement of Corporate Intent (a GBE planning document) rather than a full corporate plan, so it is comparatively high-level on initiatives and silent on budgets and procurement timing — several opportunity sizings are inferred from named activities rather than dollar figures. As a commercially-disciplined, self-funded GBE, DHA buys selectively and weighs commercial value, so offerings must be tightly scoped and ROI-led. External research (news, ANAO, Parliament) could not be completed due to tool limits in the supplied research section, so those three sections are empty and should be verified before client-facing use. Service mappings reflect the best single fit; digitisation and the KPI review are the highest-confidence, most time-bound prospects.
